titlestmt.subtitle: Papers of Frank Eugene Taylor, 1932-1984, at the Lilly Library, Indiana University
titlestmt.author: Sarah Helen Carter

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 8, 2026 at 13:58
Finding aid enhancement project. Kyra Triebold
Finding aid completed. Sarah Helen Carter

did.abstract: The Taylor, F. mss., 1932-1984, consist of the correspondence, papers, business files and memorabilia of publisher and film producer Frank Eugene Taylor, 1916-1999.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Many collections are housed offsite; retrieval requires advance notice. Please make an appointment a minimum of one week in advance of your visit.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Photography and digitization may be restricted for some collections. Copyright restrictions may apply. Before publishing, researchers are responsible for securing permission from all applicable rights holders, then filling out the Permission to Publish form.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: The Taylor, F. mss., 1932-1984, consist of the correspondence, papers, business files and memorabilia of independent publisher, and film producer Frank Eugene Taylor, 1916-1999. Born in Malone, New York, Frank Taylor graduated from Hamilton College, took some graduate psychology courses at the University of Minnesota, but soon decided upon a publishing career. He began with the New York publishers, Reynal & Hitchcock, first as a salesman, then advertising manager and publicity director, and in 1944 became editor-in-chief and vice president of the firm. Among his literary discoveries while with Reynal & Hitchcock were Lillian Smith, Arthur Miller, and Karl Shapiro. In 1948 Taylor moved to Random House as editor, scout and project director while under simultaneous contract with Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer. In 1950, after making one first-rate, award-winning, low-budget film, Mystery Street, he left Hollywood and returned to book publishing only to be asked by old friends John Huston and Arthur Miller to produce their film, The Misfits, starring Clark Gable, Marilyn Monroe, and Montgomery Clift. Taylor became publisher and executive officer of Avon Books, then a division of Hearst Corporation, in 1962 and then returned to hard cover publishing with his move to McGraw Hill two years later. During the 70s and 80s Taylor was involved in various publishing projects, acted as an independent literary agent, and worked to promote and develop art, cultural and publishing projects with China from the very beginning of the resumption of U.S./China relations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Among the correspondents in the collection are Samuel Hopkins Adams, James Agee, John Bainbridge, Alan Paul Bell, Matthew Joseph Bruccoli, Charles Spencer Chaplin, Oona (O'Neill) Chaplin, Eldridge Cleaver, Kathleen Neal Cleaver, Norman Cousins, Ralph Ellison, Richard M. Elman, Guy Endore, Leslie Aaron Fiedler, Maxwell David Geismar, Fred Lawrence Guiles, John Richard Hersey, John Huston, Clifford Michael Irving, Christopher Isherwood, Dorothy Jeakins, Lyndon Baines Johnson, Joe Klaas, Joseph Losey, Malcolm Lowry, Herbert Marshall McLuhan, Robin Maugham, Warren Miller, Desmond Morris, Vladimir Nabokov, John Pearson, Leo Calvin Rosten, Francis Russell, Dore Schary, Mark Schorer, Lillian Eugenia Smith, Stephen Spender, Ch'ing-ling Sung, Frank Eugene Taylor, and Charles Lincoln VanDoren.

Also present in the collection are files relating to the publication of autobiographies by Charlie Chaplin and Stephen Spender, manuscripts by and other materials about Eldridge Cleaver, Malcolm Lowry's unproduced film script for Tender is the Night , materials re: production of the movies The Misfits and Mystery Street, as well as several folders of research, travel notes and memorabilia, and descriptions concerning Taylor's various China projects.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into the following series: I. CORRESPONDENCE, II. TRAVEL NOTES, III. MEMORABILIA AND PERSONAL EFFECTS, IV. SPEECHES, V. PHOTOGRAPHS, VI. GENERAL SUBJECT FILES, VII. CHINA SUBJECT FILES, and VIII. PRINTED MATERIALS

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Arrangement of correspondence and preliminary searching of names done by Elissa Henken, June 1980. Remaining unprocessed materials processed by Sarah Helen Carter under the supervision of Kyra Triebold, completed in 2024.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Item], Taylor, F. mss., Lilly Library, Indiana University, Bloomington, Indiana.

did.unittitle: Report on Frank Taylor Trip (Iowa, Illinois, Minnesota)
Lyndon B. Johnson Inauguration Invitation and Order Form
Reception for Mr. and Mrs. Taylor, Guest List and Correspondence
[山村溪流 = A Stream Through a Mountain Village]

odd.head: General
odd.p: Copy of Wu Guanzhong's water ink painting, "山村溪流" (English title: "A Stream Through a Mountain Village") (1980).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Title and date information from: 吳冠中. "山村溪流 = A Stream Through a Mountain Village." In 主编水天中, 汪华 = The complete works of Wu Guanzhong, vol. 10, edited by 水天中 and 汪华. 湖南美朮出版社 (2012): 243. https://search.worldcat.org/title/189989115

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Also see "Mao Blanket" subject file (Box 4, Folder 24).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains photographs, oversized calligraphy, and certificate as well as the folder all items were originally housed together in.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Also see photograph in "Exhibitions--Museum of Chinese History" subject file (Box 4, Folder 13).

did.unittitle: National Exhibition of Chinese Book Design (全国书籍装帧艺术展览)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains handwritten notes by Frank Eugene Taylor from original folders dated October 26, 1984

did.unittitle: The Carleton Miscellany: A Review of Literature & the Liberal Arts, vol. XVIII, no. 3
Selected titles from the NEW AVON, summer and early fall trade list
The Writer in America: A Great American Critic Sums up His Views on a Nation's Life and Literature
The Wild Duck: An Authoritative Text Edition of a Great Play
The Legacy of Leonardo: Italian Renaissance Paintings from Leningrad
古城西安: Ancient City of Xi An
中国儿童画选: Selected Drawings by Chinese Children
Wushu!: The Chinese Way to Family Health & Fitness

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes note that reads "This is the only book that came out of all my negotiations with the Chinese. -- A nothing book if there ever was one 10/26/84"
